title: Section 25
content: When the day on which a promissory note or bill of exchange is at maturity is a public holiday, the instrument shall be deemed to be due on the next preceding, business day. Explanation.-- The expression "public holiday" includes Sundays: 1*** and any other day declared by the 2[Central Government], by notification in the Official Gazette, to be a public holiday.
footnote: 1. The words New Year's day, Christmas day: if either of such days falls on a Sunday, the next following Monday: GoodFriday: omitted by Act 37 of 1955, s. 3 (w.e.f. 1-4-1956). 2. Subs by the A.O. 1937, for L.G.
